questions.question: Where should you actually chat with the agent? This decides the whole redesign. (All three still need the SC3 wave to give the agent working browser tools — that part is separate.)

options.label: Embed web chat in side panel
options.description: The side panel renders the dicopilot.net chat in an iframe, reusing your existing web-app login (no token juggling), while the agent drives the attached tab via the relay. Single polished surface WITHOUT a new auth model. Main risk: cross-origin iframe cookies/CSP in the extension — fiddly but avoidable.
options.preview:
┌─ DI Copilot ──────────┐
│  [ web-app chat here  ]│  ← iframe, uses
│  [ full features      ]│    your login
│                        │
│ Agent Browser: ● ON    │  ← relay controls
│ Attached: LinkedIn tab │
└────────────────────────┘
Reuses login · no new token

options.label: Native side-panel chat
options.description: Build real chat into the side panel itself. Most control over the UX, but needs a backend auth change: issue a chat-capable credential to the extension (broaden/extend the browser_agent token). Security-sensitive — I'll STOP-gate that change for your approval before building.
options.preview:
┌─ DI Copilot ──────────┐
│  You: research this    │  ← native chat,
│  Agent: ...            │    streams direct
│                        │
│ Agent Browser: ● ON    │
│ Attached: LinkedIn tab │
└────────────────────────┘
Needs backend auth change

options.label: Web app is the cockpit
options.description: Keep the extension as a pure relay: attach a tab, then chat at dicopilot.net; the agent drives your tab. Least new code. But it keeps the two-window juggle you said feels unpolished, and the side-panel Chat tab should be removed to stop confusing you.
options.preview:
Browser tab: dicopilot.net
┌────────────────────────┐
│  chat with agent here  │
└────────────────────────┘
        +
Extension side panel =
just relay status/attach
(no chat tab)
